articles.title: Which football rule changes will be implemented during the World Cup?
articles.summary: Red cards for covering mouths, corner kicks for time wasting and broadened use of VAR technology will be implemented.

articles.summary: The US citizens allege that they have been denied entry to Britain due to their outspoken criticism of Israel.

articles.title: Vinicius brings Maracana curtain-raiser to life as Brazil rout Panama 6-2
articles.summary: Christian Pulisic breaks goal drought as US beat Senegal 3-2 in international friendly ahead of World Cup.

articles.title: Washington proposes ‘roadmap’ for de-escalation in Lebanon: US official
articles.summary: Plan aims to create conducive environment for a gradual de-escalation and cessation of all hostilities, official says.

articles.summary: While a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, Israel has deepened its offensive into southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Tense election night in Colombia as country heads to presidential runoff
articles.summary: Colombia’s election heads to a June 21 runoff after a tight first round between de la Espriella and Cepeda.

articles.title: Israel airstrike kills at least two Palestinians at Gaza port
articles.summary: At least two Palestinians were killed and around a dozen wounded when an Israeli air strike hit a crowded cafe.

articles.title: Ex-US Fed Chair Powell warns against politicisation amid Trump’s attacks
articles.summary: Jerome Powell says the US central bank is undergoing a 'stress test' like other institutions in the current era.

articles.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has accused the United States of interfering in Mexico’s internal affairs.

articles.title: US says ban on AI chip shipments applies to Chinese firms outside China
articles.summary: Department of Commerce issues guidance on chip restrictions amid concerns about loopholes in export control regime.

articles.title: ‘Before, the land sustained us’: Who benefits from Guinea’s bauxite wealth?
articles.summary: The country has vast reserves of the ore, a source material for aluminium. But citizens still languish in poverty.

articles.summary: Far-right outsider, Abelardo de la Espriella, will face leftist senator, Ivan Cepeda on June 21.

articles.title: Cepeda, de la Espriella advance in Colombia’s presidential election
articles.summary: The left-wing senator and far-right newcomer will face each other in a run-off on June 21, with security a top issue.

articles.title: Nicaragua confirms death in custody of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera
articles.summary: Rivera spent nearly three years in detention, cut off from the outside world, leading advocates to fear for his welfare.

articles.title: Can AI cure loneliness? South Korea’s robot companions for seniors
articles.summary: South Korea is using AI-powered companion dolls to help tackle loneliness in its ageing population

articles.title: Ethiopia to vote in first elections since Tigray peace deal
articles.summary: Four years after the formal end of Ethiopia's Tigray war, Ethiopians will be voting in general elections.

articles.title: Kohli powers Bengaluru to ‘stuff of dreams’ back-to-back IPL titles
articles.summary: Having never previously won the Indian Premier League, Virat Kohli seals consecutive titles as Gujarat Titans defeated.

articles.title: As Ethiopia votes, its deepening human rights crisis must be addressed
articles.summary: Continuing internal conflicts and deepening repression are casting a long shadow over the general election.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s election is about affirming national commitment to democracy
articles.summary: A national vote alone can't address all challenges in the country, but it can be a step toward democratic consolidation.

articles.title: Newark mayor imposes curfew at Delaney Hall immigration detention centre
